title: expand_vars: wasted alloc when no substitution
description: evaluator.rs expand_vars(): when the input contains '$' or '\' the function allocates out=String::with_capacity and fills it char-by-char, then returns Cow::Borrowed(s) if modified==false (i.e. nothing actually changed). The fully-filled out is then dropped — wasted heap allocation. Fix: delay construction of out until the first substitution is actually about to occur, pushing everything before the trigger point into out at that point. Alternatively, just always return Cow::Owned(out) when out has been built, removing the modified flag entirely, accepting the difference in pointer identity but not correctness.
